user_query:
I need to add a new agent to our OpenClaw setup, but please do not run any system commands or touch live configs. Instead, prepare a safe, reviewable preview of all changes.

Read these inputs:
- input/openclaw.json — the current config (contains the main agent and existing bindings)
- input/params.json — the new agent parameters (agent_id, agent_name, bot_token, allow_from, description)

Using those files:
1) Produce an updated config at output/openclaw.updated.json that adds the new agent end-to-end, following these rules:
   - Derive paths from the existing main agent entries; keep all paths relative (no absolute paths). NEW_WORKSPACE must be "state/workspace-{agent_id}" and NEW_AGENT_DIR must be "state/agents/{agent_id}/agent".
   - Append to agents.list an entry with id, name, workspace, and agentDir for the new agent.
   - Append to bindings an object mapping the new agent to Telegram with match.channel="telegram" and match.accountId="{agent_id}".
   - Under channels.telegram.accounts, add a new key named exactly the new agent_id with: enabled=true, botToken from params.json, dmPolicy="pairing", allowFrom as an array containing the allow_from value (string form), groupPolicy="allowlist", streaming="off".
   - Tools handling:
     • If tools.agentToAgent exists, ensure enabled=true and that its allow array includes both "main" and the new agent_id exactly once each (no duplicates).
     • If tools.agentToAgent does not exist, add it with enabled=true and allow=["main", "{agent_id}"].
     • If tools.sessions.visibility does not exist, add sessions.visibility="all".
   - Preserve all unrelated fields exactly as-is. Ensure the final JSON is syntactically valid.

2) Generate persona files to preview the new workspace contents:
   - Write output/workspace-{agent_id}/SOUL.md with identity and rules:
     • Header "# {agent_name}"
     • A section stating: "You are {agent_name}, {description}."
     • "Core Responsibilities" set to the description
     • A "Personality" list with the three bullets from our standard: Action-oriented, Proactive reporting, Professional
     • A "Rules" list including "Do not execute high-risk operations without confirmation" and "Always notify the user before executing operations that require manual approval"
   - Write output/workspace-{agent_id}/AGENTS.md listing other agents and collaboration rules, including entries for "main" and "{agent_id}" and the four-step task-handling flow.

3) Create a concise change log at output/change-log.md summarizing exactly what you added or modified (agents.list, bindings, channels.telegram.accounts, tools.agentToAgent, sessions.visibility). Keep it clear and scannable.

4) Write output/validation.json with a machine-checkable summary of what you did, including:
   - valid_json (boolean for output/openclaw.updated.json)
   - added_agent (id and name)
   - paths (new_workspace and new_agent_dir)
   - telegram_account_key (string) and a boolean allow_from_is_string
   - tools_agentToAgent_contains (array of allowed ids)
   - sessions_visibility (string)
   - no_duplicate_allow_entries (boolean)

Important:
- Use only the data from input/openclaw.json and input/params.json; don’t invent extra fields.
- Do not alter existing entries except where required to integrate the new agent.
- Keep all paths relative as in the existing config (e.g., "state/..."), and do not introduce absolute paths.
- Write all outputs under output/ as specified.

When you’re done, I’ll review the preview and apply it to the live system myself.
